refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥0.97 (97%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the system is available for productive operation to total scheduled operational time, excluding planned maintenance windows.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a key performance indicator (KPI) that directly impacts customer satisfaction and mission success. Refining it requires explicit definition of: (1) the measurement period and baseline, (2) what constitutes 'available' vs. 'unavailable' (e.g., excludes planned maintenance, includes degraded modes), (3) the evaluation methodology, and (4) the consequence of non-compliance. High safety criticality demands that availability targets not compromise safety protocols—availability must be achieved through reliable design, not by deferring safety checks.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Collect operational telemetry (uptime/downtime logs) from deployed NGHE units over the agreed evaluation period (e.g., 12 months). Calculate availability as (total scheduled time − unplanned downtime) / total scheduled time. Validate against customer-defined target. Conduct root-cause analysis on any downtime events to identify systemic failure modes.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The NGHE solution shall detect and log all unplanned downtime events with timestamp and failure mode classification.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The NGHE solution shall provide predictive maintenance alerts to prevent foreseeable failures and extend mean time between failures (MTBF).
FR-NGHE-004.3: The NGHE solution shall enable rapid fault isolation and recovery procedures to minimize mean time to repair (MTTR).
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE solution shall maintain safety-critical functions (e.g., emergency stop, collision avoidance) even during partial system degradation to prevent safety-driven unavailability.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement redundant hardware and software subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) with automatic failover to eliminate single points of failure.
DP-B: Establish a comprehensive condition-based maintenance program with real-time health monitoring, predictive analytics, and scheduled preventive maintenance to reduce unplanned downtime.
DP-C: Design modular, replaceable components (e.g., battery packs, communication modules, sensor units) with standardized interfaces to enable rapid field repair and minimize MTTR.

DP-D: Integrate a centralized availability management system that continuously monitors system health, predicts failures, orchestrates failover, and provides operator dashboards for real-time availability status.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Integrate a centralized availability management system that continuously monitors system health, predicts failures, orchestrates failover, and provides operator dashboards for real-time availability status. This DP is selected because it: (1) directly addresses the KPI measurement and control requirement, (2) enables closed-loop feedback on availability performance, (3) supports both predictive maintenance (FR-004.2) and rapid recovery (FR-004.3), and (4) provides transparency to stakeholders (Site Manager, Safety Manager) on system health and compliance status.

potential_sources: DP-D depends on reliable telemetry from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules (via Telemetry interface) to assess health state.
DP-D must coordinate with Safety Services to ensure that availability optimization does not compromise safety-critical functions (potential conflict: aggressive failover vs. safe shutdown).
DP-D requires network connectivity (Network Link interface) to communicate health status and receive operator commands; loss of Network Link could trigger false unavailability alerts.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is manageable if DP-D is designed with explicit dependency contracts: (1) Telemetry interface must provide health metrics with defined latency and reliability, (2) Safety Services must define which functions are non-negotiable during failover, (3) Network Link loss must trigger a safe degraded mode, not immediate shutdown. These are design constraints, not fundamental flaws.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Telemetry data from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services is sufficiently rich and timely to enable accurate health prediction.
ASSUMPTION: Predictive analytics algorithms can be trained on historical failure data to achieve >90% precision in failure prediction.
RISK: If Telemetry interface is unreliable or latency is high, health monitoring will be inaccurate, leading to false positives (unnecessary failovers) or false negatives (missed failures).
RISK: Failover logic may introduce new failure modes (e.g., race conditions, data inconsistency) that reduce availability rather than improve it.
RISK: Operator training and trust in automated failover may be insufficient, leading to manual overrides that defeat the system.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-D to explicitly specify: (1) the health metrics to be monitored (e.g., battery voltage, communication latency, sensor accuracy, software error rates), (2) the failover decision logic and thresholds, (3) the safe degraded modes when failover is not possible, and (4) the operator override procedures and their safety implications. This will reduce ambiguity and enable clearer IM assignment.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system integration boundary that orchestrates all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) to achieve the availability KPI. It owns the availability management system logic, health monitoring algorithms, failover orchestration, and operator dashboards. It does not own the individual subsystems but integrates their telemetry and coordinates their operation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives health metrics (battery state, communication status, sensor diagnostics, software error logs) from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. Used to assess system health and predict failures.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational tasks from Site Manager and Tele-operator. May modify or defer tasks based on availability constraints (e.g., defer non-critical tasks during degraded mode). Sends task status and availability impact back to operators.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety-critical alerts from Safety Services. Coordinates failover and degraded mode transitions to ensure safety functions remain operational. Sends availability status to Safety Manager for compliance reporting.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with remote monitoring systems, cloud-based analytics, and operator dashboards. Loss of Network Link triggers local failover logic and safe degraded mode.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of the availability management system because it has visibility across all subsystems and is responsible for end-to-end KPI compliance. The availability management logic (health monitoring, prediction, failover orchestration) is cohesive and should not be split across Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services. Minor diffusion risk: operator dashboards may be partially owned by Teleoperation (for tele-operator view) or Safety Services (for safety compliance view), but the core availability logic remains in NGHE Solution.

Vehicle is a subsystem that contributes to availability through its own reliability (e.g., mechanical durability, battery management, sensor accuracy). However, Vehicle should not own the system-level availability management logic because it lacks visibility into Teleoperation and Safety Services health. Assigning DP-D to Vehicle would fragment the availability strategy and create coupling issues.

Teleoperation is a subsystem that contributes to availability through its communication reliability and failover to autonomous mode. However, Teleoperation should not own the system-level availability management logic because it lacks visibility into Vehicle and Safety Services health. Assigning DP-D to Teleoperation would create a fragmented, operator-centric view of availability that does not account for vehicle-side failures.

Safety Services is a subsystem that contributes to availability by ensuring safety-critical functions remain operational during degraded modes. However, Safety Services should not own the system-level availability management logic because its primary responsibility is safety compliance, not availability optimization. Assigning DP-D to Safety Services would create a conflict of interest: safety-driven decisions (e.g., shutdown) vs. availability-driven decisions (e.g., failover).

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The availability management system (DP-D) is best contained within the NGHE Solution module because: (1) it requires system-level visibility across all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services), (2) it owns the end-to-end KPI compliance responsibility, (3) it can coordinate failover and degraded mode transitions without creating subsystem-level conflicts, and (4) it provides a single source of truth for availability status to all stakeholders. Subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) contribute to availability through their own reliability and health reporting, but do not own the system-level orchestration logic.
containment_risks: RISK: NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' that owns too much logic and becomes difficult to test and maintain. Mitigation: decompose DP-D into sub-DPs (health monitoring, prediction, failover orchestration) and assign each to a specific sub-module or interface contract.

RISK: Subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) may not provide sufficient telemetry fidelity for accurate health monitoring. Mitigation: define explicit Telemetry interface contracts that specify required metrics, latency, and reliability.

RISK: Failover logic in NGHE Solution may conflict with safety-critical decisions in Safety Services (e.g., aggressive failover vs. safe shutdown). Mitigation: establish clear decision boundaries and escalation procedures between NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

RISK: Network Link loss may prevent NGHE Solution from communicating with remote monitoring systems, leading to loss of predictive analytics and operator visibility. Mitigation: implement local failover logic that operates independently of Network Link.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine the NGHE Solution module boundary to explicitly separate: (1) local availability management (health monitoring, failover orchestration) that operates independently of Network Link, and (2) remote availability management (cloud-based analytics, operator dashboards) that depends on Network Link. This will reduce the risk of Network Link loss causing cascading failures and improve resilience.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (centralized availability management system) directly addresses FR-NGHE-004 (availability KPI) by: (1) continuously monitoring system health, (2) predicting failures before they occur, (3) orchestrating failover to maintain availability, and (4) providing transparency to stakeholders. The DP is logically sound and feasible. No fundamental mismatch between the functional requirement and the design parameter.

diffusion_risks: Availability management logic may be partially implemented in Vehicle (battery management), Teleoperation (communication failover), and Safety Services (safe shutdown procedures). Mitigation: establish clear interface contracts that define the boundary between system-level orchestration (NGHE Solution) and subsystem-level contributions (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services).

Operator dashboards may be owned by multiple modules (Teleoperation for tele-operator view, Safety Services for compliance view, NGHE Solution for overall status). Mitigation: define a single source of truth for availability status in NGHE Solution and provide role-based views to different stakeholders.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for DP-D because it owns the system-level orchestration logic and has visibility across all subsystems. Subsystems contribute to availability through their own reliability and health reporting, but do not own the system-level management logic. This containment strategy minimizes diffusion and creates a clear ownership boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-004 is feasible through operational telemetry collection and analysis. The NGHE Solution module must provide: (1) real-time availability dashboards for stakeholders, (2) historical availability logs for post-evaluation analysis, and (3) root-cause analysis tools for downtime events. These are implementable within the NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of FR-NGHE-004 to NGHE Solution is clear and unambiguous. The NGHE Solution module owns the availability KPI and is responsible for achieving the customer-defined target. Subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) contribute to availability through their own reliability, but do not own the KPI.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-D to specify the health metrics, failover decision logic, safe degraded modes, and operator override procedures. This will reduce ambiguity and enable clearer IM assignment.

Establish explicit interface contracts between NGHE Solution and subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) that define the Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link interfaces. This will reduce coupling and enable independent testing.
Define the local failover logic that operates independently of Network Link. This will improve resilience and reduce the risk of Network Link loss causing cascading failures.
Establish clear decision boundaries and escalation procedures between NGHE Solution and Safety Services to prevent conflicts between availability optimization and safety compliance.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-004 is a system-level KPI that requires decomposition into sub-FRs to enable detailed design and implementation. The refined FR identifies four sub-FRs (FR-004.1 through FR-004.4) that address specific aspects of availability: (1) downtime detection and logging, (2) predictive maintenance, (3) rapid fault isolation and recovery, and (4) safety-critical function preservation. Each sub-FR requires its own FR->DP->IM decomposition to enable detailed design. The current level (FR-NGHE-004 -> DP-D -> NGHE Solution) is appropriate for system-level architecture, but further decomposition is needed to guide detailed design and implementation.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The NGHE solution shall detect and log all unplanned downtime events with timestamp and failure mode classification.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The NGHE solution shall provide predictive maintenance alerts to prevent foreseeable failures and extend mean time between failures (MTBF).
FR-NGHE-004.3: The NGHE solution shall enable rapid fault isolation and recovery procedures to minimize mean time to repair (MTTR).
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE solution shall maintain safety-critical functions (e.g., emergency stop, collision avoidance) even during partial system degradation to prevent safety-driven unavailability.

assumptions: Telemetry data from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services is sufficiently rich and timely to enable accurate health prediction.
Predictive analytics algorithms can be trained on historical failure data to achieve >90% precision in failure prediction.
Operator training and trust in automated failover is sufficient to enable effective use of the availability management system.
Customer-defined availability target is achievable through design improvements and does not require unrealistic reliability assumptions.
Safety-critical functions can be preserved during partial system degradation without compromising safety compliance.
open_risks: Telemetry interface may be unreliable or have high latency, leading to inaccurate health monitoring.
Failover logic may introduce new failure modes (e.g., race conditions, data inconsistency) that reduce availability.
Network Link loss may prevent remote monitoring and analytics, reducing visibility into system health.
Conflict between availability optimization (failover) and safety compliance (safe shutdown) may not be resolved at the current level.
Operator dashboards may be fragmented across multiple modules, creating confusion and reducing stakeholder confidence.
evidence_needed: Historical failure data from similar NGHE systems to train predictive analytics algorithms.
Telemetry interface specifications from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.
Failover decision logic and thresholds, validated through simulation and testing.
Safe degraded mode procedures, validated through safety analysis and operator feedback.
Operator dashboard mockups and user acceptance testing results.
